rubric_en: A growing number of charismatic Christians see themselves as waging a spiritual battle against the forces of Satan. Sometimes those forces are right across the street.
rubric_zh: 越来越多的灵恩派基督徒认为自己正在与撒但的势力进行属灵争战。那些势力有时就在街对面。

paragraphs.en: From the outside, the church looked like a plain brick storefront, the mirrored windows peeling, a sign above painted white with blue letters. THE WELL, it read, and underneath, REVIVAL HUB.
paragraphs.zh: 从外面看，这座教堂像一间普通的红砖店面，镜面窗户剥落了漆，门口上方有一块白底蓝字的牌子。上面写着“THE WELL”，下面写着“REVIVAL HUB”。

paragraphs.en: There were older and grander churches in Maryville, a college town in East Tennessee where you could barely drive a minute without passing a cross or a sign about Jesus. But when Mike and Andrea Brewer established the Well, in 2016, they understood themselves to be part of something more mystical and revolutionary than any existing denomination—a charismatic-Christian movement that has drawn millions of Americans with the promise of supernatural encounters with God and visions of cosmic battle.
paragraphs.zh: 在位于田纳西东部的大学城玛丽维尔，有许多更古老、更宏伟的教堂，你几乎开车一分钟就会经过一个十字架或关于耶稣的标语。但当迈克和安德里亚·布鲁尔在2016年创办“THE WELL”时，他们认为自己属于一种比任何既有教派都更神秘、更革命性的运动——一种吸引了数百万美国人的灵恩派基督教运动，向人们承诺可以与上帝发生超自然相遇，并描绘宇宙大战的景象。

paragraphs.en: By his own account, Mike had been an exhausted factory worker and a lapsed Pentecostal addicted to pornography when one night, at home and praying for a better life, he heard an unfamiliar voice calling out to him and believed that it was God. At church a few days later, he would write, he felt a “tangible explosion” in his chest, followed by “the purity and righteousness of God moving through me in waves.” He came to believe that a demon had exited his body and that the Holy Spirit had taken its place. He decided that God had chosen him for a divine assignment.
paragraphs.zh: 据他本人说，迈克原本是个疲惫不堪的工厂工人，也是一个离群的五旬节派信徒，而且沉迷于色情。直到有一个晚上，他在家祈祷、盼望生活好转时，听到一个陌生的声音呼唤他，并相信那是上帝的声音。几天后在教堂里，他写道，自己感到胸口有“真实的爆裂感”，接着是“上帝的纯洁与公义如浪潮般在我体内流动”。他于是相信有一个恶魔离开了他的身体，而圣灵取而代之。他决定上帝已为他指定了一个神圣使命。

paragraphs.en: The Brewers began attending conferences with names such as “Voice of the Prophets” and “Voice of the Apostles” in places like Lancaster, Pennsylvania, and Springfield, Missouri. At one gathering, Mike claimed to have seen an actual angel, and at another, a manifestation of the Holy Spirit that he described to me as “like five fog machines, like a cloud just rolling into the room.” He and Andrea came to believe that God was unleashing new signs and wonders and raising up modern-day apostles and prophets, including, it turned out, them.
paragraphs.zh: 布鲁尔夫妇开始参加一些会议，名字像“先知之声（Voice of the Prophets）”和“使徒之声（Voice of the Apostles）”，地点在宾夕法尼亚州兰开斯特和密苏里州斯普林菲尔德等地。在一次聚会中，迈克声称自己看见了一位真正的天使；在另一次聚会中，他看到圣灵的显现，他向我形容那景象像“五台雾机一样”，像一团云正滚进房间。他和安德里亚于是相信，上帝正在释放新的神迹奇事，并正在兴起当代的使徒与先知，其中当然也包括他们。

paragraphs.en: Andrea and Mike Brewer, the founders of the Well, consider themselves hardened spiritual warriors. (Houston Cofield for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: Well 的创始人安德烈（Andrea）和迈克·布鲁尔（Mike Brewer）把自己视为久经锻炼的属灵战士。（Houston Cofield 供图，题图刊登于《The Atlantic》）

paragraphs.en: They went abroad as missionaries to India and Haiti, which only confirmed their emerging understanding of a universe with three distinct realms—the heavenly, the earthly, and the underworld, with the Earth being the realm of spiritual warfare. On one side, the Holy Spirit, angels, and believers comprised an army of God. On the other were the forces of Satan—legions of demons with names, ranks, and personalities that could inhabit people, geographical regions, and entire nations. In India, the Brewers claimed to have battled Shiva, Brahma, and Kali. In Haiti, Python and Mami Wata. There was Marduk, Osiris, Ra, Horus, Diana, Artemis, Shesha Naga, and so on—a whole pantheon of demons that represented ancient religions and civilizations, and whose earthly expressions were essential to understanding current events.
paragraphs.zh: 他们曾作为传教士前往印度和海地，这反而印证了他们逐渐形成的一种世界观：宇宙分为三个不同的领域——天堂界、地上界和冥界，地球是属灵战争的前线。一边是圣灵、天使和信徒，组成了上帝的军队；另一边是撒但的势力——成群成群的恶魔，拥有名字、等级和性格，能够寄居在个人、地理区域乃至整个国家之中。在印度，布鲁尔夫妇声称与湿婆（Shiva）、梵天（Brahma）和迦梨（Kali）作过斗争；在海地，则与皮同（Python）和玛米·瓦塔（Mami Wata）对抗。还有马尔杜克（Marduk）、奥西里斯（Osiris）、拉（Ra）、荷鲁斯（Horus）、戴安娜（Diana）、阿耳忒弥斯（Artemis）、Shesha Naga 等等——这是一整个神祇谱系般的恶魔群，被视为代表着古代宗教和文明，其在地上的化身对于解读当下事件至关重要。

paragraphs.en: By the time the Brewers returned to Maryville, they saw themselves as hardened spiritual warriors. They founded the Well to continue the battle, joining an international network of churches and ministries called Global Awakening, which also had a seminary, where Andrea began studying demon history and hierarchies. When Mike asked God for their exact assignment, he told me when I visited in March, “the Lord spoke so clearly. He said, ‘I’m giving you and the Well a mandate for the full eradication of witchcraft and demonic activity in the region.’”
paragraphs.zh: 等布鲁尔夫妇返回马里维尔时，他们已将自己视作久经锤炼的属灵战士。他们创办了 Well，以继续这场战斗，并加入了名为“全球觉醒”（Global Awakening）的国际教会和事工网络，该网络还设有一所神学院，安德烈在那里开始研究恶魔的历史与等级制度。迈克在我三月访问时问神是否为他们指定了具体使命时告诉我道：“主说得非常清楚。祂说，‘我把你们和 Well 一同托付给你们，这是你们对该地区进行彻底根除巫术和恶魔活动的使命。’”

paragraphs.en: And that was what led the Brewers to look across the street one day a few years later and determine that the central hub for demonic activity in the region was roughly 100 yards away. It was a bookstore called Southland.
paragraphs.zh: 正是这份使命，促使布鲁尔夫妇几年后有一天抬头望向街对面，认定该地区恶魔活动的核心据点大约就在百码之外。那是一家名为 Southland 的书店。

paragraphs.en: The owner was Lisa Misosky, and she was chatting with customers one afternoon when she found out that people in town were accusing her of demonic activity, and not in a metaphorical way.
paragraphs.zh: 这家书店的老板是丽莎·米索斯基（Lisa Misosky），有一天下午她正在和顾客聊天，突然发现镇上的人指责她从事“恶魔活动”，而且这不是比喻式的说法。

paragraphs.en: Over the course of three decades in Maryville, Misosky had made Southland Books and Cafe into a local institution, a sprawling maze of old bookcases where people could find a leather-bound Mark Twain, a paperback Charles Bukowski, shelves of military history, and flyers for a local mah-jongg group. Misosky had a bar downstairs where she hosted trivia nights, readings, all-ages punk shows, and fundraisers that sometimes involved drag performances. She occasionally provided space to the local Democratic Party. But none of that had drawn public protest until a new church moved in across the street.
paragraphs.zh: 在玛丽维尔生活了三十余年，米索斯基把南地书屋与咖啡馆（Southland Books and Cafe）打造成了当地的一处地标，这里像一座蔓延的旧书架迷宫，顾客可以找到皮质精装的马克·吐温、平装版的查尔斯·布考斯基、军事史书架，以及本地麻将社团的宣传单。米索斯基楼下有一个酒吧，她在那里举办问答之夜、读书会、全龄段朋克演出，以及有时会涉及脱口秀表演的募款活动。她也会偶尔把场地借给本地民主党。可是，直到对面街道出现一所新教堂之前，这些都没有引起公众抗议。

paragraphs.en: “You’re not gonna believe this shit,” a friend texted her, and then sent the first of several videos posted by a man who introduced himself as Mike Brewer, the leader of an “apostolic hub” called the Well. Sitting at a desk, he explained in a calm and methodical manner that the bookstore had been identified as a “regional demonic stronghold.” A high-ranking demon named Lilith was involved, Misosky would learn, and the bookstore was being targeted for something called “strategic-level spiritual warfare,” the goal of which was to “remove the enemy.”
paragraphs.zh: “你绝对不会相信这件事，”一位朋友给她发来短信，并接着发来由一名自称为迈克·布鲁尔（Mike Brewer）的男子发布的第一段视频。布鲁尔是一个名为“井恩使徒圣工中心”（"the Well"）的“使徒联盟基地”（apostolic hub）的负责人。他端坐在桌前，平静而有条理地解释说，这家书店已被认定为一个“地区性恶魔据点”。米索斯基后来了解到，名为莉莉丝（Lilith）的高级恶魔卷入其中，这家书店正被列入所谓“战略级属灵战争”的目标，而这场“战争”的目的就是“消除敌人”。

paragraphs.en: Lisa Misosky, the owner of Southland Books and Cafe (Houston Cofield for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 南地书屋与咖啡馆老板丽莎·米索斯基（Houston Cofield，发表于《大西洋月刊》）

paragraphs.en: Misosky had been born and raised in Maryville. She was 58, Catholic, and gay, and told me she was used to living among conservative Christians. Still, demonic came as a surprise. “This is probably the dumbest thing I’ve ever heard,” she remembered thinking after seeing the first video, not yet realizing that the church was part of the fastest-growing segment of Christianity in the country, or that the language she was hearing in the fall of 2022 was spreading across the Christian right and the wider political landscape.
paragraphs.zh: 米索斯基出生并长大在玛丽维尔。她今年 58 岁，是天主教徒，也是同性恋，告诉我她早已习惯生活在保守基督徒中。尽管如此，听到“恶魔”仍然让她意外。“这大概是我听过的最蠢的一句话，”她回忆起看了第一段视频后的想法。她当时还没有意识到，这家教堂是美国基督教增长最快的人群之一，也没有意识到她在 2022 年秋天听到的这种说法正在基督教右派和更广泛的政治领域快速传播。

paragraphs.en: Molly Worthen: What the growth of charismatic Christianity reveals about America
paragraphs.zh: 莫莉·沃森：魅力型基督教增长对美国的启示

paragraphs.en: In the years ahead, Donald Trump would accuse the entire Democratic Party of being demonic. Tucker Carlson would claim that he had been mauled by a demon in his sleep. Steve Bannon would call Lutheran and Catholic activists who help immigrants demonic. A federal emergency-management official would speak of being teleported to a Waffle House 50 miles away, elaborating that he was not sure whether the transporting forces were “good” or “evil.” J. D. Vance would say of UFOs, “I don’t think they’re aliens. I think they’re demons.” And the same apostles and prophets who’d claimed that God had anointed Trump to be president would encourage him to see his war with Iran as a cosmic showdown with a demonic entity known as the Prince of Persia.
paragraphs.zh: 未来几年里，唐纳德·特朗普会指责整个民主党都“如恶魔般腐败”。塔克·卡尔森会说他在睡梦中被一个恶魔“撕咬”。斯蒂夫·班农会称那些帮助移民的路德宗和天主教活动人士为恶魔。联邦应急管理官员会谈到自己被“传送”到50英里外的一家华夫屋（Waffle House），并进一步说，他不确定将他传送过来的是“善”还是“恶”的力量。J. D. Vance 会在谈到不明飞行物时说：“我不认为它们是外星人。我认为它们是恶魔。” 那些曾宣称上帝膏立特朗普成为总统的同一批使徒和先知，也会鼓励他把自己与伊朗的战争视为与一个名为“波斯王子”的恶魔般存在的宇宙级对决。

paragraphs.en: In that beginning moment, though, Misosky was simply wondering what the accusations meant for her bookstore and the people who went there. Why was she being targeted? What, precisely, was demonic about Southland? The mah-jongg? The romantasy section? A drag performer called Icky Stardust? Her? She wondered if she needed to worry about security.
paragraphs.zh: 然而在那个开始的瞬间，米索斯基只是想知道这些指控对她的书店和来店里的人意味着什么。为什么她会被点名？南地书店的哪一点才是“恶魔化”的？她心里在想，是麻将馆吗？是浪漫奇幻专区吗？还是一个叫“Icky Stardust”的变装艺人？还是她本人？她在想自己是不是该担心安全问题。

paragraphs.en: She began searching for books on the subject, learning of an entire specialty called demonology. She found a manual written by an East Texas couple called Pigs in the Parlor: A Practical Guide to Deliverance, which had a chapter outlining 53 different demonic groupings.
paragraphs.zh: 她开始翻找相关书籍，了解到有一个完整的学科叫做“恶魔学（demonology）”。她找到了一本由一对德州东部夫妇写的手册《Pigs in the Parlor: A Practical Guide to Deliverance》，其中有一章列出了 53 种不同的“恶魔分组”。

paragraphs.en: From her front door, she kept an eye on what was happening across the street. A tobacco store blocked her full view of the church, but on Thursdays and Sundays, she could see cars and trucks wheeling into the craggy parking lot.
paragraphs.zh: 从她的前门，她能看到街对面正在发生什么。一个烟草店挡住了她对教堂的完整视线，但在星期四和星期天，她能看到汽车和卡车转入那块崎岖的停车场。

paragraphs.en: In one way, of course, none of this was new. Belief in satanic forces has been part of Christianity since the first century. What was relatively new was the rising movement that was supercharging these concepts, and that had first taken root in charismatic circles during the 1990s. Early leaders called their ideas the New Apostolic Reformation, claiming that a wave of Holy Spirit power was surging around the globe, heralding a “new apostolic age.” NAR leaders revised a common End Times narrative in a way that would prove revolutionary: Instead of retreating from the world and awaiting the return of Jesus Christ, they believed, Christians were supposed to establish God’s Kingdom, right now, on Earth.
paragraphs.zh: 从某种意义上说，这些想法当然不是什么新鲜事。自第一世纪起，基督教中就一直存在对撒旦势力的信仰。相对较新的，是一个正在兴起的运动把这些观念推向新高度，而它最早是在1990年代在感召派圈子里扎根。早期领导人把自己的理念称为“新使徒改革运动”，称全球正在掀起一阵圣灵能力的浪潮，预示着“新的使徒时代”的来临。新使徒改革运动（NAR）的领导人重塑了一个常见的末世叙事，进而产生革命性影响：他们认为基督徒应该不是退守世外、等待耶稣基督再来，而是当下就在地上建立上帝的国度。

paragraphs.en: From the February 2025 issue: Stephanie McCrummen on the New Apostolic Reformation’s army of God
paragraphs.zh: 摘自2025年2月号：Stephanie McCrummen 对新使徒改革运动之“上帝军队”专题报道

paragraphs.en: Their version of the Kingdom mapped neatly onto the political goals of social conservatives, libertarians, and, more recently, the MAGA movement. The Kingdom would have limited government, free markets, two genders, one kind of marriage, and one kind of God. The “right now” part, meanwhile, offered an urgent paradigm for mobilizing grassroots believers out of the Church and into electoral politics, government, education, and all other realms of life where they were to assert God’s dominion. The new apostles and prophets of the NAR spread these ideas through decentralized networks of churches, international prayer ministries, schools, revivals, and prayer rallies, attracting followers who could find a sense of power and purpose in building the Kingdom. Leaders spoke of believers as “warriors” or “God’s army” or even “special forces,” and churches as “military bases,” and certain apostles as “generals.” They believed that being a Christian meant being in a constant state of spiritual warfare.
paragraphs.zh: 他们的“天国”设想与社会保守主义者、自由主义者，以及更近来的“MAGA”运动的政治目标完美对应。这个“天国”会有“极小化政府、自由市场、两个性别、一种婚姻、一个上帝”。而“现在行动”这一部分，则提供了一个紧迫的框架，把教会中的基层信徒动员出来，进入选举政治、政府、教育以及生活的所有其他领域，在那里他们要体现上帝的权柄。新纳米（NAR）的使徒和先知们通过去中心化的教会网络、国际祈祷事工、学校、复兴运动和祈祷集会传播这些观念，吸引了那些能在建立“天国”中感受到力量和目标感的人。领袖们称信徒为“战士”“上帝的军队”甚至“特种部队”，把教会称作“军事基地”，把某些使徒称作“将军”。他们相信，做基督徒意味着要处在持续不断的属灵争战状态中。

paragraphs.en: In its most basic form, this simply meant praying for God to eradicate evil. But NAR leaders pioneered a more radical version that they called “strategic spiritual warfare,” which entailed the idea that demons could take over cities and institutions, and that Christians could target and scatter them by their physical presence, intensive prayer, singing, marching, and other strategies.
paragraphs.zh: 在最基本层面，这只是指为上帝祈求除去邪恶。但NAR的领袖们开创了一种更激进的说法，称为“战略性属灵争战”，其核心在于：恶魔能够占据城市和机构，而基督徒可以凭借身体在场、集中祷告、唱歌、游行等策略，瞄准并驱散这些恶魔。

paragraphs.en: One version of how this could look was when a team of NAR leaders in the ’90s climbed Mount Everest, where they spent weeks praying at various altitudes in an attempt to displace a high-ranking demon called the Queen of Heaven, whom they believed to be suppressing the spread of Christianity across the Middle East and Asia. Another version was the run-up to the January 6, 2021, insurrection, when prominent apostles and prophets held prayer rallies calling for “the minutemen of the Kingdom” to rise up against demonic forces that they believed had stolen the 2020 election, after which many of their followers were among those who stormed the U.S. Capitol. Another version was what happened after the Brewers returned to Maryville.
paragraphs.zh: 这种做法的一种体现是，1990年代一支NAR领袖小组登上珠穆朗玛峰，在不同海拔高度祈祷了数周，试图驱逐一名被称为“天堂皇后”的高阶恶魔。他们相信这位恶魔在中东和亚洲压制基督教扩展。另一种体现发生在2021年1月6日政变事件前，知名的使徒和先知们组织了祈祷集会，号召“王国的民兵”起来反抗他们认为窃取了2020年选举的恶魔势力，之后他们许多追随者便成为参与冲击美国国会大厦的人群之一。第三种体现则是布鲁尔家族（Brewers）回到玛丽维尔之后发生的事。

paragraphs.en: The first gatherings of the Well were held in the office of a former used-car dealership, just a few families praying for God to reveal the enemy. As more people joined, the group relocated to an office building, where one Sunday Mike decided that it was time to begin the first phase of strategic spiritual warfare, targeting what he considered to be ground-level demons. He invited anyone carrying “emotional pain or tormenting thoughts” to come to the front of the room. As he and Andrea tell the story, a young man recently released from prison stepped forward. Andrea put her hands on his shoulders, and as Mike ordered any demons to come to attention, the young man began shaking and crying, a catharsis that Mike declared to be God’s victory over a demon that they later identified as Odin, because of the man’s participation in a white-supremacist prison gang that embraced the Norse god. After that, more people began coming forward, and it was during this time that Mike received the mandate for the full eradication of witchcraft and demonic activity in the region.
paragraphs.zh: “井”组织的第一次聚会在一家前二手车行的办公室里举行，最初只有几个家庭祈祷，请求上帝显明敌人。随着更多人加入，团体迁到了一个办公楼。一堂礼拜中，迈克决定是时候开始“战略性属灵争战”的第一阶段，目标是他认为的“基层恶魔”。他邀请所有“带着情绪痛苦或折磨性想法”的人走到前面。正如迈克和安德烈娅所叙述，一名最近出狱的年轻人走了出来。安德烈娅把手放在他肩上，迈克命令所有恶魔“起来注意”，那名年轻人开始发抖并哭泣，米克宣布这是一场大洗礼式宣泄，是上帝战胜了一位他们后来认定为“奥丁”的恶魔——因为这名男子曾参与一个崇拜北欧神奥丁的白人至上主义囚犯帮派。就在此后，更多人开始走出来，这段时期里，迈克也得到了在该地区“全面消灭巫术和恶魔活动”的使命。

paragraphs.en: Services at the Well, in Maryville, Tennessee, are typical of those in charismatic churches—people dancing with prayer flags, pacing the room, lying prostrate on the floor. Thursday evenings are set aside for delivering people from demons. (Houston Cofield for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 田园教会（The Well）在田纳西州玛丽维尔的聚会，和许多灵恩派教会的聚会一样——人们拿着祈祷旗跳舞，在会场里踱步，甚至全身趴在地板上。周四晚上专门用于“驱魔”救赎（Houston Cofield 为《大西洋》杂志拍摄）。

paragraphs.en: The church moved in 2021 to the brick building, a former grocery store along a main thoroughfare in town, and set aside Thursday nights for delivering the people of Maryville. The sign went up. Into the sanctuary went 100 or so chairs arranged in a semicircle around a drum kit, guitars, and amplifiers. On one wall went a map of the area superimposed with what appeared to be a huge spiderweb that divided the region into prayer sectors. Where a church’s pulpit or a cross would usually be was an arrangement of amber-toned spotlights and glowing flameless candles. And when people came, they found the sort of free-form services common in the movement—people dancing with colored prayer flags, or pacing the room, or lying prostrate on the floor, the band playing one anthemic song after another.
paragraphs.zh: 该教会于2021年搬进一栋砖造建筑，这座旧杂货店坐落在镇上的一条主干道上，并把周四晚上定为为玛丽维尔居民施行释放仪式的时间。告示牌也随之挂起。礼拜堂里摆上了大约100把椅子，半圆形围绕着一套鼓组、吉他和音箱。过去通常放讲坛或十字架的位置，被一组琥珀色聚光灯和发光的无火蜡烛取代。每当有人到场，他们就会见到这种灵恩派常见的自由形式崇拜：有人拿着彩色祈祷旗子起舞，有人来回踱步，有人俯身趴在地板上，乐队则接连演奏一首又一首颂歌。

paragraphs.en: A woman named Sasha, a driver for Uber Eats at the time, told me that on her first night at the Well, “this cry came out of me,” which she believed was a demon leaving her body, freeing her of the emotional and physical pain of a hysterectomy. A 62-year-old woman named Pam told me that a deliverance team conducted a “spiritual evaluation” to determine how demons might have entered her body, asking whether she’d had astral projections or tarot cards read, done meditation or yoga, or ever felt envious, angry, depressed, or insecure. The team then led her through an elaborate process of renouncing curses and revoking demonic rights, and when it was over, she said, “I really believed I was a child of God.” A young man suffering from severe anxiety and depression told me that after his cleansing, he felt “the most love I have ever felt.” A middle-aged woman who had struggled with drug and pornography addictions told me that after several sessions, she felt “euphoric—whole, complete, one, merged with the Trinity,” and that whatever God asks of her, “I will do, at all costs.”
paragraphs.zh: 当时是一名名叫莎莎的女性给我讲述说，她在优步外卖（Uber Eats）开车时来到“井”教会。她说，第一次来这间教会的那个夜晚，“我就发出了这样的哭喊”，她认为那是恶魔离开她身体的表现，使她从子宫切除手术带来的情绪与身体疼痛中解脱。62岁的帕姆告诉我，释放小组为她做了“属灵评估”，以确定恶魔可能如何进入她的身体，并询问她是否做过星体投射、塔罗牌占卜，是否做过冥想或瑜伽，或者是否曾经感到过嫉妒、愤怒、抑郁或不自信。然后小组带她经过一套复杂程序，放弃诅咒并撤销魔鬼的“权利”，结束时，她说：“我真的相信自己是上帝的孩子。”一名年轻男性，长期受重度焦虑和抑郁困扰，告诉我在完成“洁净”后，感到“迄今为止我体验到过的最深的爱”。一位中年女性曾与药物和色情成瘾作斗争，她告诉我在几次会面后，感到“欣快——完整、圆满、统一，和三位一体合而为一”；并说无论上帝向她提出什么要求，“我都会去做，哪怕付出一切代价。”

paragraphs.en: People came from Alabama, Florida, Michigan, Minnesota. Mike said that a wealthy businessman from Indiana flew in for a private session. Deliverance leaders told me that they identified demons by the ways a person might be tormented. Feelings of worthlessness was Belial. Sexual confusion could be Jezebel. Anger could be Thor, who was under the command of Odin. The Brewers estimated that they’d delivered many hundreds of people, enough that they decided they were ready to move into the next phase of spiritual warfare. This meant identifying higher-ranking territorial demons, which involved a process that Andrea called determining the “narrative of hell” over the region.
paragraphs.zh: 聚会者来自阿拉巴马州、佛罗里达州、密歇根州和明尼苏达州。迈克说，一位来自印第安纳州的富商特意飞来，进行私人会谈。释放领袖告诉我，他们会根据一个人可能遭受的折磨方式来识别恶魔。空虚和无价值感被视为贝利尔。性困惑可能是耶洗别。愤怒可能是托尔——他受奥丁指挥。酿酒师们估计自己已经释放了许多上百人，足够他们认为已经可以进入灵性战争的下一阶段。这意味着要识别更高阶层、管辖地盘的恶魔，过程涉及安德烈亚称之为“确定该地区地狱叙事”。

paragraphs.en: She began gathering what she called “spiritual intelligence.” She kept track of what demons had been identified during deliverances at the Well. As she drove around the area, she told me, she made note of Masonic lodges, tarot-card readers, and anything else that made her feel uneasy. She paid attention to her dreams. Then came the day that Mike noticed an event posted on Facebook, an upcoming fundraiser for local foster kids that involved death-metal and drag performances. The Murvul Punk Toy Drive was being held at a bookstore right across the street.
paragraphs.zh: 她开始收集她所谓的“属灵情报”。她记录下在“井”这间场所的赶鬼过程中被识别出的魔鬼。在她开车在附近转悠时，她会把看到的共济会会所、塔罗牌占卜师，以及任何让她感到不安的事物都记下来。她也很注意自己的梦。后来有一天，迈克在 Facebook 上看到了一个活动公告：一场为本地寄养孩子筹款的活动，会有死亡金属和变装表演。MURVUL Punk玩偶募捐活动（Murvul Punk Toy Drive）要在街对面的一家书店举行。

paragraphs.en: The Brewers had not heard much about Southland, but scrolling through the shop’s Facebook page, they saw rainbow flags. They saw postings about local Democratic Party meetings and a drum circle, along with videos of past drag shows, including one in which Icky Stardust performed an elaborate routine to a blasting metal version of “White Wedding” and poured fake blood all over their dress. Mike thought that he saw a teenager in the audience.
paragraphs.zh: 酿造者（Brewers）夫妇对南方国（Southland）并不太熟悉，但在浏览这家商店的 Facebook 页面时，他们看到彩虹旗。他们看到了关于地方民主党会议和鼓乐圈的帖子，还看到过往变装秀的视频，其中一场表演里，“Icky Stardust”跳了一套复杂的舞蹈，伴随着激烈的金属版《White Wedding》，并把假血泼满了自己的裙子。迈克觉得他看到观众席上有一位十几岁的少年。

paragraphs.en: “It was obvious,” Andrea said. The high-ranking demon influencing the region was Lilith, a Mesopotamian wind goddess who ruled over forbidden sexual desire, and Southland was the stronghold, which Mike defined as a place where certain thoughts, attitudes, and behaviors contrary to God were flourishing. Mike informed the church elders. “I said, ‘We cannot just let this stuff go,’” he told me. “I said, ‘This is evil.’”
paragraphs.zh: “这很明显，”安德烈亚说。她说，影响这个地区的高级恶魔是莉莉丝（Lilith），一位美索不达米亚的风之女神，主宰着被视为禁忌的性欲。南方国是它的根据地，迈克将“根据地”定义为：一些与上帝相悖的思想、态度和行为正在繁荣的地方。“我告诉教会长老：‘我们不能就这样放着不管。’”他对我说，“我说：‘这就是邪恶。’”

paragraphs.en: After she watched the video, Misosky started asking around about the Brewers. She was a little bit older than Mike, but it only took a couple of phone calls to find out that he was from a nearby town called Townsend, known as a tourist gateway to the Great Smoky Mountains. Her sister and a friend had worked with his cousins; another friend who had worked at Southland had joined the Well, and Misosky had not seen her since. She realized that Andrea had worked at a local hardware store.
paragraphs.zh: 看完视频后，米索斯基开始四处打听关于酿造者夫妇的情况。她比迈克大一点，但只打了几个电话就查到他来自附近一个叫汤森德（Townsend）的镇，这个镇被称为大烟山（Great Smoky Mountains）的旅游入口。她的妹妹和一个朋友曾与他的表亲共事；另一个在 Southland 工作过的朋友已经加入了“井”，米索斯基之后就没见过她了。她意识到安德烈亚以前在本地的一家五金店上过班。

paragraphs.en: “Jackleg preacher,” Misosky would mutter when Mike’s name came up.
paragraphs.zh: “半吊子传道人，”每当Mike的名字被提起时，Misosky就会低声嘀咕。

paragraphs.en: She had never wanted to be a political activist. But during the Trump era, Southland was becoming a social haven of the sort that can be found in many small southern towns. The campus of Maryville College was down the street, and students and faculty often hung out in the café. A professor sometimes gave lectures on the Constitution. Older gay couples met for beers. Misosky decided to host the county’s first Pride event in 2019, the same year that the Well received the mandate. She had thought that a dozen or so people would show up, but more than 700 did, which she found unexpectedly moving. She hosted more events after that, including drag-show fundraisers; minors could attend with a parent or a guardian, which was the case with the punk toy drive that was now drawing the attention of the Well.
paragraphs.zh: 她从来没想过要成为一名政治活动者。但在特朗普时代，南方郡逐渐成为一种社会庇护所，这种情形在许多南方小镇都能看到。玛丽维尔学院的校园就在街对面，学生和教职员工经常在咖啡馆里闲逛。有一位教授有时会给他们讲宪法课。年长的同性伴侣会在这里碰杯喝啤酒。Misosky决定在2019年主办该县首次Pride活动，那一年，Well也拿到了许可。她原以为会有十几个人前来参加，但结果来了700多人，这让她意外地很受感动。随后她又主持了更多活动，包括脱口秀筹款夜；未成年人可以在父母或监护人陪同下参加，这在如今引起Well关注的朋克玩具义卖活动中也得到了实行。

paragraphs.en: Misosky flipped through Pigs in the Parlor. “This is the day of spiritual battle and spiritual-victory,” read a chapter titled “The Final Conflict.” “The warfare is on!”
paragraphs.zh: 她翻阅《客厅里的猪》。题为“最终决战”的一章写道：“这是属灵争战与属灵胜利的一天。” “战争已经打响！”

paragraphs.en: She went online and looked up Lilith. “Primordial she-demon,” read one description. “Banished from the Garden of Eden for disobeying Adam,” read another.
paragraphs.zh: 她上网查找了莉莉丝。“原初的雌魔，”一条解释是这样写的。“因违抗亚当而被放逐出伊甸园，”另一条则写道。

paragraphs.en: “Every story needs a hero—a protagonist and an antagonist,” she remembered thinking. “So I guess I’m their antagonist, in collusion with Lilith.”
paragraphs.zh: “每个故事都需要一个英雄——一个主角和一个反派，”她想起自己当时是这么想的。“所以我猜我是他们的反派，与莉莉丝勾结在一起。”

paragraphs.en: She wanted to blow it all off but couldn’t. She’d thought that the QAnon conspiracy was bonkers, and it had compelled a man to drive to Washington, D.C., with an AR-15 and fire shots inside a pizza parlor. Nancy Pelosi, as speaker of the House, had been called demonic, and then her husband had been assaulted by a man who spoke of “evil” forces.
paragraphs.zh: 她想把这一切都当成一场可有可无的事，但做不到。她曾认为“QAnon”阴谋论离谱得离谱，却促使一名男子驾驶AR-15前往华盛顿特区，并在一家比萨店里开火。作为众议院议长的南希·佩洛西曾被称为“恶魔”，之后她的丈夫又遭到一名提到“邪恶”势力的男子攻击。

paragraphs.en: Across the street, the Brewers turned a conference room at the Well into what they called a “war room.” They put maps of all the surrounding counties on the walls, representing what they considered their spiritual theater of operation. Mike began posting about the fundraiser to his thousands of social-media followers, saying that it was from “the pits of hell.” At some point, he said, someone apparently upset by this sent him an envelope full of excrement; others sent checks and urged him to keep going. A few congregants started “prayer walking” near the bookstore, a tactic of spiritual warfare that had been deployed in the days and weeks before the January 6 insurrection, when people marched around the U.S. Supreme Court and state capitol buildings, calling the Holy Spirit into battle.
paragraphs.zh: 在街对面，布鲁尔夫妇把威尔的一个会议室改造成了他们所谓的“作战室”。他们在墙上挂上了周边所有县的地图，作为自己所谓的属灵行动战场。迈克开始向他成千上万的社交媒体追随者发布筹款消息，说这笔钱来自“地狱深处”。在某个时候，似乎有人因此生气，给他寄来一封装满粪便的信封；也有一些人寄来支票，并敦促他继续下去。一些会众开始在书店附近进行“祈祷行走”，这是一种灵战策略，在1月6日暴动前后几天和几周内也曾被使用，当时人们围绕美国最高法院和州议会大楼游行，在那里呼唤圣灵参战。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, Andrea began doing research into Tennessee laws and found an old statute banning cabaret performances within 1,000 feet of a church. The Brewers sent the information to the local district attorney, the police, the sheriff, and city and county commissioners. Soon, a wider circle of activists and pastors became involved in the cause, including one who had recently held a book burning in the town of Mt. Juliet, about three hours away—a huge bonfire that had drawn a crowd of cheering people who’d tossed copies of Harry Potter and other books deemed demonic into the flames.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，安德烈开始研究田纳西州的法律，并发现了一条旧法令：禁止在教堂1000英尺范围内进行歌舞表演。布鲁尔夫妇将该信息提交给了当地地方检察官、警察、治安官以及市和县委员。随后，更多活跃分子和牧师加入了这场运动，其中包括一位不久前在离镇子约三个小时车程的Mt. Juliet举行过书籍焚烧的人——那是一场巨大的篝火晚会，吸引了一群欢呼的人群，他们把《哈利·波特》以及其他被视为“魔鬼化”书籍的版本扔进火中。

paragraphs.en: At the Well, Mike showed images of the drag shows to his congregation. He kept streaming live videos describing what was happening at Southland as “wicked.” Then, a few days before the fundraiser, in November 2022, Mike and Misosky spoke by phone.
paragraphs.zh: 在“At the Well”时，Mike 向他的会众展示了那些变装表演的图片。他持续直播并把Southland发生的事情称为“邪恶”。然后，在2022年11月，距离募款活动还有几天时，Mike 和 Misosky 通了电话。

paragraphs.en: Her recollection is that she called Mike, and that he talked about doing spiritual warfare against voodoo chiefs in Haiti, and that she said, “That’s great, Mike. Why don’t you have a cup of coffee with me?”
paragraphs.zh: 她的回忆是，她打了Mike的电话，Mike谈到要对海地的巫毒头目进行灵性战争。她随后说：“太好了，Mike。你为什么不和我来杯咖啡呢？”

paragraphs.en: Mike’s recollection is that he called Misosky. “I said, ‘I’m not calling to resolve differences. We’re not going to do that. I am calling to request that you go 18 and older for events,’” he said. “ ‘If you’re marketing to children of this area, we’re going to do everything within the law and the spirit to stop you. We will never harm you physically.’ I said, ‘I’m calling out of respect.’”
paragraphs.zh: Mike的回忆是，他是给Misosky打的电话。“我说：‘我打电话来不是为了解决分歧。我们不会那样做。我打电话来是为了请你们把活动限定在18岁及以上人群参加。’ 他又说：‘如果你们在向这一地区的孩子做宣传，我们将依法并以所有可以动用的精神力量全部阻止你们。我们绝不会对你们造成身体伤害。’ 我说：‘我之所以打这个电话，是出于尊重。’”

paragraphs.en: And that was the last time they spoke.
paragraphs.zh: 那就是他们最后一次交谈。

paragraphs.en: Soon after, Misosky got a call from the Anti-Defamation League. There had been some chatter about a protest at Southland on neo-Nazi forums that the group monitored. At the time, all kinds of LGBTQ events around the country were being targeted by extremist groups. A gunman had just killed five people and injured 19 at an LGBTQ nightclub in Colorado. Misosky called the police. She called some local pastors she knew and asked that they show up at the fundraiser wearing their collars. She posted on Facebook that “MAGA fascists” were threatening the toy drive.
paragraphs.zh: 不久之后，米索斯基接到反诽谤联盟（ADL）的来电。该组织监测到新纳粹论坛上有人在讨论在Southland的抗议活动。那时，全国各地的各种 LGBTQ 活动都在被极端组织盯上。一名枪手刚刚在科罗拉多一间 LGBTQ 夜总会开枪，打死 5 人、重伤 19 人。米索斯基报警了。她给她认识的一些当地牧师打电话，请他们穿着教会领带前来筹款活动现场。她在 Facebook 上发布消息说，“MAGA 法西斯分子”正在威胁玩具募捐活动。

paragraphs.en: The next evening, she stood with dozens of supporters in front of the bookstore, watching as a group of nine men, some with their faces covered with bandannas, marched down a side street and up to the sidewalk in front of the entrance, where police stopped them from going any farther. According to local press reports, the men held signs that read GROOMERS ARE PEDOPHILES and IT’S OK TO BE WHITE. At least one of them appeared to have a gun.
paragraphs.zh: 第二天晚上，她与几十名支持者站在书店前，看到一群九名男子从侧街走过来，其中一些人用头巾把脸遮住，走到入口前的人行道上。警方在他们继续前进之前把人拦下了。根据当地媒体报道，这些人举着“改造论者是恋童癖”和“当白人就很好”两块牌子。至少有一人似乎携带了枪。

paragraphs.en: The Brewers said that they had no idea who the protesters were, and that they were at home at the time. On Facebook, Mike speculated that Misosky had organized a “false flag” operation to smear Christians. Later, the ADL and another watchdog group would identify the men as members of a neo-Nazi group called the Tennessee Active Club, part of a network of such clubs across the United States. Members of the group would show up the next year in the town of Franklin, about three and a half hours from Maryville, to provide security for a mayoral candidate named Gabrielle Hanson. During her campaign, Hanson spoke of battling vague forces threatening the nation and was anointed for office at a tent revival whose promotional posters declared WE ARE TAKING BACK THE LAND BY DISPLACING DEMONIC FORCES AND USHERING IN HIS GLORY. (Hanson said in a statement later that she had not hired the men who showed up, and denied being affiliated with “any white supremacy or Nazi-affiliated group.”)
paragraphs.zh: 布鲁尔斯夫妇表示，他们根本不知道抗议者是谁，那天他们还在家里。穆克在 Facebook 上猜测，米索斯基组织了“假旗行动”来抹黑基督徒。后来，ADL 和另一家监察机构确认，这些男子是名为 Tennessee Active Club 的新纳粹组织成员，它隶属于美国各地类似俱乐部组成的一张网络。该组织的成员次年出现在离马里维尔约三个半小时车程的富兰克林镇，为一位名叫加布里埃尔·汉森（Gabrielle Hanson）的市长候选人提供安全保障。在竞选期间，汉森曾谈到要与威胁国家的各种模糊势力作斗争，并在一场帐篷布道活动中接受按立任命，该活动的宣传海报上写着“我们正在夺回土地，以驱逐恶魔力量，并迎来他的荣耀”。（汉森后来在声明中说，她没有雇佣出现的这些人，并否认与“任何白人至上主义或纳粹相关组织”有任何关联。）

paragraphs.en: In front of Southland, there was shouting back and forth between the protesters and Misosky’s supporters, and after a few hours, the masked men returned to their cars, which were parked several blocks away; some of them reported to the police that their tires had been slashed.
paragraphs.zh: 在 Southland 门前，抗议者与米索斯基的支持者互相高喊着。几个小时后，这些戴面具的人又回到停放在几条街外的车辆旁；其中一些人向警方报称，他们的轮胎被人割伤了。

paragraphs.en: The fundraiser went on, but Misosky remained unsettled. She blamed the Well for “putting a target on our back” and providing “moral cover” for people who might want to justify violence. She spent the night of the protest and several nights after that camped out on the floor of Southland with a.38, a 9-mm, a shotgun, and a baseball bat.
paragraphs.zh: 募款活动仍在继续，但米索斯基始终心神不宁。她指责“这口井”在“把我们背后挂上了靶子”，并为可能想为暴力行为辩护的人提供了“道德掩护”。她在抗议当晚以及之后几个晚上都在Southland的地板上过夜，手里拿着一支.38手枪、一支9毫米手枪、一支猎枪和一根棒球棒。

paragraphs.en: Mike told me that he and Andrea were merely bringing the reality of demonic activities at Southland to light. “The truth hurts,” he said. “We won’t resolve our differences, our worldviews. I would never physically harm anyone, but I will bring an awareness.”
paragraphs.zh: 迈克告诉我，他和安德烈亚只是把Southland发生的“恶魔活动”真相公开出来。“真相是会伤人的，”他说。“我们不会解决彼此的分歧和世界观问题。我绝不会对任何人施加身体伤害，但我会提高人们的警觉。”

paragraphs.en: When I reached one of the protesters named in local press accounts, he told me that he had never heard of the Well but made clear his view of drag performances. “Influencing children to sexual activities isn’t demonic?” he wrote in a text message.
paragraphs.zh: 当我联系到当地媒体报道中提到的一位抗议者时，他告诉我自己从未听说过“这口井”，但他清楚地表达了自己对拖曳表演的看法。“让孩子接触性行为不是恶魔行为吗？”他在短信中写道。

paragraphs.en: Not long after the fundraiser, Misosky received word from the county sheriff, through an intermediary, asking whether she wanted to sign her staff up for active-shooter training.
paragraphs.zh: 募款活动结束不久后，米索斯基通过一名中间人收到了县警长的消息，问她是否想让员工报名参加“主动枪击者”应对训练。

paragraphs.en: Andrea, meanwhile, received word from God. It came through one of her mentors, a prophet in Colorado, who trained people in spiritual warfare and told Andrea that she had gotten a prophecy that the Well was entering into a final battle with Lilith, which the Brewers understood less as a prediction than as an instruction.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，安德里亚从上帝那里得到了消息。消息通过她的一位导师传来，这位导师是科罗拉多的一位先知，专门训练人们进行属灵争战。那位先知告诉安德里亚，她得到了一个预言：韦尔（Well）正在与莉莉丝（Lilith）进行一场终局之战。对布鲁尔一家来说，这更像是一项指示，而不是单纯的预言。

paragraphs.en: Until recently, all of this might have been considered a dispatch from the fringes of American religion, except that the ideas taking hold in Maryville are becoming more and more mainstream.
paragraphs.zh: 直到最近，这一切都可能只是被视为美国宗教边缘地带的消息，但玛丽维尔（Maryville）正在流行的这些思想，正在越来越主流化。

paragraphs.en: While the Southern Baptists, United Methodists, and other denominations continue to decline, millions of Americans are finding their way to nondenominational churches with names such as Oasis, Elevation, and Harvest Rock, where they are learning about the intricacies of demons, spiritual warfare, and other NAR ideas. Some of the nation’s largest megachurches, such as El Rey Jesús in Florida and Free Chapel in Georgia, are led by apostles and prophets in the movement. One such apostle, Paula White-Cain, is President Trump’s spiritual adviser.
paragraphs.zh: 当南方浸信会、卫斯理宗以及其他宗派持续式微时，数百万美国人正在涌向诸如“绿洲教会”（Oasis）、“以色列高地”（Elevation）和“收割岩场”（Harvest Rock）等无宗派教会，在那里他们正在学习有关恶魔、属灵争战以及新使徒运动（NAR）其他观念的复杂内容。美国一些最大的巨型教会，如佛罗里达州的El Rey Jesús和佐治亚州的Free Chapel，都由该运动中的使徒和先知领导。其中一位使徒，保拉·怀特-凯恩（Paula White-Cain），是特朗普总统的灵性顾问。

paragraphs.en: Read: The most interesting part of Trump’s prayer rally
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：特朗普祈祷集会中最有意思的部分

paragraphs.en: By 2024, roughly 61 percent of American Christians agreed with the statement that “there are modern-day apostles and prophets,” and roughly half agreed that “there are demonic ‘principalities’ and ‘powers’ who control physical territory,” according to a survey conducted by Paul Djupe, a Denison University political scientist who is among the few scholars attempting to track the ways that NAR ideas are transforming Christianity. By December 2025, roughly 59 percent of evangelical Christians and 22 percent of non-evangelicals agreed that “the church should organize campaigns of spiritual warfare and prayer to displace high-level demons,” Djupe found in a follow-up survey.
paragraphs.zh: 到2024年，约61%的美国基督徒赞同“如今仍有现代使徒和先知”这一说法，并且大约一半的人认同“有恶魔的‘统治权’和‘势力’在掌控某些具体地域”，这一点出现在德尼森大学政治学家保罗·朱佩（Paul Djupe）的一项调查中。该调查追踪的是少数学者正在尝试研究“新使徒与先知运动”（NAR）思想如何改变基督教现状的方式。根据其后续调查，到了2025年12月，约59%的福音派基督徒和22%的非福音派基督徒赞成“教会应组织属灵争战和祷告行动，以驱逐高层恶魔”。

paragraphs.en: The same survey also indicated that more people are encountering these concepts on social media than in church, which speaks to how people are following apostles and prophets through online ministries and prayer networks, and to how influencers across the broader Christian right are leveraging these ideas to gain and possibly radicalize followers.
paragraphs.zh: 同一份调查还显示，比起在教会里，更多人是在社交媒体上接触到这些概念，这说明人们正通过线上事工和祷告网络追随使徒与先知，也说明更广泛的基督教右派中的意见领袖正利用这些想法来吸引并可能激进化追随者。

paragraphs.en: This was obvious at the memorial for Charlie Kirk following his assassination last year. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth described the moment at hand as a “spiritual war.” The right-wing influencer Benny Johnson urged members of Trump’s Cabinet to “wield the sword for the terror of evil men in our nation.” The far-right activist Jack Posobiec told people to “put on the full armor of God and face the evil in high places and the spiritual warfare before us,” rhetoric that has only continued to escalate as the midterm elections approach.
paragraphs.zh: 这种现象在去年查理·柯克（Charlie Kirk）遭遇暗杀后的追悼会上表现得很明显。国防部长皮特·赫格塞斯（Pete Hegseth）将当时的时刻形容为“属灵战争”。右翼意见领袖本尼·约翰逊（Benny Johnson）呼吁特朗普（Donald Trump）内阁成员“挥起神剑，镇压我们国家中邪恶之人的恐怖”。极右翼活动家杰克·波斯比克（Jack Posobiec）则告诉人们“穿上上帝的全副军装，面对高处的邪恶并进行眼前的属灵战争”，这种言辞在中期选举临近之际只会进一步升级。

paragraphs.en: Read: Charlie Kirk and the ‘third Great Awakening’
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：查理·柯克与“第三次大觉醒”

paragraphs.en: Speaking about the anti-ICE protests in Minneapolis earlier this year, the influential NAR strategist Lance Wallnau said that “the demons are manifesting.” More recently, the lieutenant governor of Indiana, Micah Beckwith, speaking about Democratic attempts to gerrymander congressional districts, said that the party was being led by “the minions and voices of darkness.”
paragraphs.zh: 今年早些时候谈及明尼阿波利斯的反ICE抗议活动时，具影响力的NAR战略家兰斯·沃尔瑙（Lance Wallnau）说“恶魔正在显现”。更近一步，印第安纳州副州长米卡·贝克维斯（Micah Beckwith）在谈论民主党试图对国会选区进行地图劫持（gerrymander）时说，民主党是被“黑暗的爪牙和黑暗的声音”所操控。

paragraphs.en: “Wake up, Christians. They are coming for you,” he said on a show called FlashPoint, a kind of nightly news for the apostles-and-prophets crowd. “You can’t pet a demon. I know people like to say, ‘Hey, demons, stay over there. Just don’t hurt us, and we won’t hurt you.’ It doesn’t work that way. Evil will find you. Until strong men stand up and do something and fight fire with fire, then we will continue to lose ground, our children will be warped, the curse will be over the land.”
paragraphs.zh: “醒醒吧，基督徒。‘他们’要来找你们了，”他在《FlashPoint》节目中说道，这是一个专门面向“使徒与先知”群体的每晚新闻节目。“你不能和恶魔友好相处。我知道有人喜欢说，‘嘿，恶魔，待在那边。只要你别伤害我们，我们也不会伤害你。’但这不管用。邪恶会找到你。只有等到强有力的人站出来，做点什么，以火还火，我们才不会继续失去阵地，我们的孩子将会被扭曲，诅咒就会笼罩这片土地。”

paragraphs.en: As the MAGA coalition has fractured, some of Trump’s former supporters have been turning this language against him. Among others, Tucker Carlson has questioned whether Trump could be the anti-Christ, while Carlson’s critics have suggested that Carlson himself might be under demonic influence. The conservative writer Rod Dreher, seeking to explain his former friend’s growing anti-Semitism, recently wrote that he wondered whether there was “some demonic force in the New England wood where Tucker lives, and if it has been working on his mind.”
paragraphs.zh: 随着MAGA联盟的瓦解，特朗普的一些前支持者开始把这种语言反过来用在他身上。其中，塔克·卡尔森（Tucker Carlson）就曾质疑特朗普是否可能是反基督，而卡尔森的批评者则认为卡尔森本人可能正处于邪灵影响之下。保守派作家罗德·德雷尔（Rod Dreher）最近在解释这位前友人的反犹激增时写道，他想知道“塔克居住的新英格兰森林里是否有某种恶魔般的力量，以及它是否在作用于他的思想。”

paragraphs.en: Dreher, a friend of Vance’s who identifies as Eastern Orthodox, has been writing a lot about demons lately and told me that he believes something larger is going on in American culture. “I think the whole materialist paradigm we’ve lived by is breaking down,” he said. “The world is becoming re-enchanted, whether people want it to be or not. It’s all very real. People—the overclass, the professional class—just don’t see it and don’t want to see it.” In books and interviews, Dreher has been promoting parable-like stories about demons that seem designed to reach those people, or perhaps shift some spiritual Overton window. One involves a haunted McMansion in Louisiana. Another is about a wealthy New York City woman whose husband placed her under an exorcist’s care. Yet another is about a Chicago lawyer terrorized by alien visitations that turned out to be demons.
paragraphs.zh: 德雷尔是温斯（Vance）的朋友，自称东正教徒，近来写了很多关于恶魔的文章，并告诉我他认为美国文化中正在发生更大规模的事情。“我认为我们一直以来所遵循的整个唯物主义范式正在瓦解，”他说，“世界正在重新被‘赋魔’，不管人们愿不愿意。那都是真的。人们——上层阶级、职业阶层——就是看不见，也不想看见。”在书籍和采访中，德雷尔一直在传播类似比喻的恶魔故事，似乎就是为了接近那部分人群，或者至少是为了移动某种精神层面的“奥弗顿窗口”。其中一个故事是关于路易斯安那州一栋闹鬼的豪宅；另一个是关于一位纽约市富裕女性，她的丈夫把她交给了一位驱魔师照料；还有另一个则讲述一位芝加哥律师遭遇外星人拜访，最后这些拜访被证实其实是恶魔。

paragraphs.en: From the March 2026 issue: Rod Dreher thinks the Enlightenment was a mistake
paragraphs.zh: 来自2026年3月期：罗德·德雷尔认为启蒙运动是个错误。

paragraphs.en: In a more immediate sense, invoking demons can be a means of dehumanizing and delegitimizing political enemies, which has often been a precursor to actual warfare and political violence. “It’s a very useful way to get around the Christian imperative to love your enemy,” Matthew Taylor, a visiting scholar at the Center on Faith and Justice at Georgetown University, told me. “For the most part, that is the ironclad command. So how do you get Christians to hate their enemies? Or to hate them even if they love them? Demons are easy to hate. They are irredeemable objects of hatred.”
paragraphs.zh: 从更直接的意义上说，提及恶魔可以成为一种去人性化并使政治对手被去正当化的手段，这往往是实际战争与政治暴力的前奏。乔治城大学信仰与正义中心的访问学者马修·泰勒告诉我：这“是绕开基督徒必须爱敌人的命令的一个非常有效的方法。就基督信仰而言，这几乎是铁律。那你如何让基督徒憎恨他们的敌人？或者即使他们本应爱他们，也让他们恨起来？恶魔很容易被憎恨。恶魔是不可得救的仇恨对象。”

paragraphs.en: In 2021, the Well moved into an abandoned grocery store across the street from Southland. (Houston Cofield for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 2021年，The Well 搬进了对面南方街一处废弃的杂货店。（《大西洋月刊》拍摄：Houston Cofield）

paragraphs.en: Which Misosky understood as she told the sheriff’s office that, yes, she would like to sign up for active-shooter training.
paragraphs.zh: 米索斯基理解了这一点，因为她告诉警长办公室，她是愿意报名参加主动枪手应对训练的。

paragraphs.en: And so, three years into a campaign of spiritual warfare against demonic forces in Maryville, she and her staff learned how long it would take for police to arrive should she call 911, and what to do in those minutes. They learned how to duck and cover, and run for the exits. The trainer suggested that Misosky arm each door with something to spray in the eyes of a possible shooter. She bought some cans of wasp spray and placed them strategically near the two entrances, behind several bookcases, by the door to the downstairs space, and under the bar.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，在对玛丽维尔“恶魔势力”持续发起三年属灵战争期间，她和她的员工学会了在她拨打911后警察需要多久才会到达，以及在这几分钟里该怎么做。他们学会了趴下掩护、跑向出口。教练建议米索斯基在每扇门旁放置一些东西，在可疑射手靠近时喷向其眼睛。她买了几罐黄蜂喷雾，并把它们有计划地放在两个入口附近、几组书架后面、楼下空间的门边，以及吧台下面。

paragraphs.en: Upstairs, she kept the baseball bat and a gun under her desk, which she adjusted so that she could face the front door and scan each person who came in. Sitting there, she sometimes found herself thinking, “I know they would shoot me first.”
paragraphs.zh: 在楼上，她把一根棒球棒和一把枪放在办公桌下，并把桌子摆成可以朝向前门、并观察每一个进来的人。她坐在那里时，有时会想，“我知道他们会先朝我开枪。”

paragraphs.en: Across the street, the Brewers were feeling more and more triumphant. In 2023, Tennessee’s state legislature passed the nation’s first law banning drag performances in public spaces or where minors could view them. Arizona, Kentucky, Oklahoma, Arkansas, and Idaho, among other states, then passed laws similar to Tennessee’s, which is being challenged in court, but which still felt to them like a victory for the Kingdom of God.
paragraphs.zh: 街对面，酿酒师小组（Brewers）越来越感到胜利在望。2023年，田纳西州议会通过了全美首个在公共场所或未成年人可观看的场合禁止变装表演的法案。随后，亚利桑那州、肯塔基州、俄克拉何马州、阿肯色州和爱达荷州等州也通过了与田纳西类似的法律。尽管这些法律正在接受司法挑战，他们仍觉得这是上帝国度的一次胜利。

paragraphs.en: In accordance with the prophecy that Andrea had received from Colorado, the Well entered the final phase of spiritual warfare against Lilith in the spring of 2024, which involved 40 days of continuous prayer asking for God to liberate Maryville. The Brewers said that people showed up every day and sometimes stayed into the night. “We just left the doors open, and people came,” Mike said, describing how he believed those prayers were answered when the state legislature passed a resolution declaring July of that year to be a month of prayer and fasting for the entire state.
paragraphs.zh: 按照安德莉亚从科罗拉多州得到的预言，灵修聚会小组（Well）在2024年春季进入了对莉莉丝最终阶段的属灵战争，这一阶段包括40天不间断祷告，祈求上帝解放玛丽维尔。酿酒师们说，有人每天都来，有时还待到晚上。“我们只是把门敞开着，然后人们就来了，”迈克说，并描述了自己如何相信当年立法机构通过一项决议，宣布当年7月为全州禁食祷告月时，那些祷告得到了回应。

paragraphs.en: That summer, a Republican state representative coordinated prayer rallies for all 95 counties across Tennessee, and Andrea was asked to speak at one in Maryville, along with state and local officials. As the Brewers saw it, the law, the government, and the whole state were coming into alignment to fulfill the mandate that God had given them.
paragraphs.zh: 那年夏天，一位共和党州议员在田纳西州全州95个县组织了祈祷集会，安德烈娅被邀请在玛丽维尔的一个集会上发言，与会者中还有州和地方官员。在酿酒厂成员看来，法律、政府以及整个州正在逐步步入同一轨道，以实现上帝赋予他们的使命。

paragraphs.en: The rally was on the steps of the county courthouse. The day was a bit cloudy, and several hundred people came, including many from the Well, some of whom had American flags in their pockets, and many of whom kneeled, then bowed, pressing their forehead to the warm concrete. When it was Andrea’s turn to speak, she felt full of God’s authority. She commanded that Lilith leave the region in the name of Jesus, at which point, she said, she heard sirens going off. When she looked up into the sky, she said, she saw something like clouds parting, and what she discerned to be a “halo” of colors in the sky.
paragraphs.zh: 祈祷会在县法院的台阶上举行。当天天气有些阴，来了几百人，其中有不少来自“井边”（Well）的信徒，其中一些人兜里揣着美国国旗，许多人跪下后又弯身伏地，将额头按在温热的水泥上。当安德烈娅轮到发言时，她感到自己充满了上帝的权威。她奉耶稣之名命令莉莉丝离开该地区，接着她说，自己听到了警报声响起。抬头望向天空时，她说自己看到像是云层分开了，在天空中看见了她所识别到的“一圈色彩斑斓的光环”。

paragraphs.en: “Something shifted,” she said. “Something changed. There was a moment where God said, ‘I am breaking through this situation.’ And everybody present—you just felt it.”
paragraphs.zh: 她说：“有些变化发生了，有些事变了。有那么一刻，神好像说：‘我正在打破这场局面。’而在场的每个人——你都能感受到。”

paragraphs.en: And now it was a spring Sunday at the Well, one like so many other Sundays in a church where spiritual warfare never really ends, and several dozen people filed into the sanctuary to hear what God might want them to do next.
paragraphs.zh: 如今是春季主日，地点同样在“井边”，和这个教会里许多其他主日并无二致，在这里属灵争战似乎从未真正结束。几十位信徒陆续走进圣堂，等待听从上帝下一步对他们的带领。

paragraphs.en: A member of the congregation gave a message about grief, but mostly there was praying as the band played, drums pounding, building and building, amber spotlights glowing. Two women danced around the room with prayer flags, and others lay on the floor. After 45 minutes of this, someone declared, “Right there, I felt like we pushed through the atmosphere,” and someone else said, “Something is breaking right now in this room, and it’s going to break through this city, and break through this region,” and someone else said, “In the name of Jesus, every demon out!”
paragraphs.zh: 一位会众成员讲了一个关于悲伤的信息，但在大部分时间里，人们都在祈祷，乐队在演奏，鼓点砰砰作响，声音一遍遍叠加，琥珀色聚光灯在发光。两个女人拿着祈祷旗在房间里绕着走，其他人则躺在地上。45分钟后， 有人宣布：“就在那一刻，我感觉我们已经冲开了那片空气，”另一个人说：“现在这房间里正有某种东西被打破，它会冲破这座城市，再冲破这个地区，”还有人说：“奉耶稣之名，所有的恶魔都退出去！”

paragraphs.en: During the two weeks I spent at the Well, prayer teams performed deliverances almost every day. Some of them involved newcomers, but many were a kind of spiritual maintenance for long-standing congregants who kept returning for more—more purification, more power, more of this version of freedom and purpose. People told me that during their deliverances, they had visions of snakes and soldiers, doors and colors.
paragraphs.zh: 在我在“真泉教会”住了两周期间，祈祷小组几乎每天都在进行“释放”仪式。它们有些为新来的人进行，但更多的是一种对长期会众的属灵维护，他们不断回来寻求更多——更多净化、更多能力、更多这种自由和人生目标的体验。人们告诉我，在他们被“释放”的过程中，会看到蛇和士兵、门和各种颜色的异象。

paragraphs.en: Sasha, the woman who’d worked for Uber Eats, told me that she had struggled with childhood trauma, homosexual feelings, and drug addiction, and that she was on her fifth or sixth deliverance to rid herself of those and other things that she did not consider God. The sessions could be emotionally exhausting, and she said that her deliverance leader had explained that she should space them out for her own safety: “She told me, ‘Honey, you’ve been through so much, your frame could not contain it.’” By that point, Sasha had been liberated from Python, Osiris, Apollo, Lilith, and other demons, and she suspected that more were still in there.
paragraphs.zh: 曾在Uber Eats工作的萨莎告诉我，她一直在与童年创伤、同性恋倾向和吸毒成瘾作斗争，并且这是她第五次或第六次“释放”仪式，目的是把这些以及她认为不属于上帝的东西赶走。这些环节可能情绪上非常消耗精力，她说她的带领者向她解释说，为了她的安全应该把这些活动分散开：“她对我说，‘亲爱的，你经历了太多，你的身体承受不了。’”到那时，萨莎已经被解脱于“皮扁蛇”“俄西里斯”“阿波罗”“莉莉丝”等恶魔，她怀疑自己体内仍有其他恶魔潜伏。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, she and others said that their deliverances were not only about their own purification. The experience had also changed how they saw the world and their role in it. “It’s like being a warrior—there’s no rest,” Sasha said. “Things are changing in the spirit realm, and people are not ready for what’s to come.”
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，她和其他人都说，这些“释放”并不只是为了自己的净化。这样的经历也改变了她们看世界的方式，以及自己在这个世界中的角色。萨莎说：“这就像当战士——没有休息。” “属灵领域正在发生变化，而人们还没有准备好迎接即将到来的事情。”

paragraphs.en: She and others told me about all the ways they now saw Satan working in the world. It was Jeffrey Epstein, and child trafficking, and underground tunnels. It was Iran, and Muslims whose goal is “to outpopulate us all and take over,” in Sasha’s words. It was Pride Month, and transgenderism. It was churches that were stifling the Holy Spirit. It was not just separation of Church and state that was the problem in this country, but a far more profound separation of humanity from what they understood to be the one true God.
paragraphs.zh: 她和其他人告诉我，如今他们看到撒但在世界上作恶的各种方式：这是杰弗里·爱泼斯坦、人口贩运，还有地下隧道。那是伊朗，是她说的那些“目标是让我们都被他们的人数超过并最终接管”的穆斯林。那是骄傲月，那是跨性别现象。那是扼杀圣灵的教会。问题不只是政教分离，而是在这个国家，人与她们认为的唯一真神之间存在更深层、更根本的隔离。

paragraphs.en: “If we keep everything separate, no one will ever see the big picture,” Sasha said, and she explained how she starts her days.
paragraphs.zh: 萨莎说：“如果我们把一切都割裂开来，谁也看不见全局。”随后她解释了自己的起床仪式。

paragraphs.en: “I wake up in the morning, and I anoint myself,” she began. She said that she asks God to open her senses to the supernatural, because at times she can smell demons. She puts her hand on her head and asks God to “silence the voice of the accuser in Jesus’s name” and to “silence the voice of my own thoughts in Jesus’s name.” She asks to “have the helmet of salvation and the very mind of Christ” and to “bring every thought into submission.” And then she heads into the world, a spiritual warrior.
paragraphs.zh: 她开始说道：“我早上醒来后，会给自己抹油。”她说自己会求神开启感官，让自己能感知超自然，因为有时她可以闻到恶魔。她把手放在头上，求神“奉耶稣的名，压制那指控者的声音”，也求神“奉耶稣的名，压制我自己思想的声音”。她请求“得着得救的头盔和基督的心意”，并“使每一个念头都顺服”。然后她便走进世界，像一名属灵战士。

paragraphs.en: Across the street, Lisa Misosky started her day with the wasp spray still by the doors, and the baseball bat and gun still under her desk, and a worn copy of Pigs in the Parlor on a shelf.
paragraphs.zh: 在隔壁的街上，莉萨·米索斯基当天的早晨则始于门边仍放着防蜂喷雾，桌子下还放着棒球棒和枪，书架上还摆着一本旧旧的《猪在客厅》副本。

paragraphs.en: The Brewers, meanwhile, had decided that their work in Maryville was done. “A beachhead has been established,” Mike said. The Well would continue. But he and Andrea were moving on to the next front, relocating to a town north of Palm Beach, Florida, where Mike was starting a ministry to train businesspeople and other leaders in “Kingdom warfare.” Andrea was developing the narrative of hell in their new neighborhood, where many streets were named for Greek gods.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，布鲁尔夫妇已经决定他们在玛丽维尔的工作已经完成。迈克说：“‘井’的前沿阵地已经建立。”‘井’将继续运作。但他和安德里亚正在转向下一个前线，搬到佛罗里达州棕榈滩以北的一座城镇，在那里，迈克将开始一项事工，训练商界人士及其他领袖进行“天国战争”。安德里亚在他们的新社区里塑造了关于地狱的叙事，那里的许多街道都以希腊神明命名。

paragraphs.en: “The Well has been in war for almost 10 years to deliver this territory,” Andrea said just before they left Maryville. “Now it’s a calmer season. But there will be another wave.”
paragraphs.zh: 就在离开玛丽维尔前，安德里亚说：“‘井’在这片领地上几乎已经作战了10年。现在是一个更平静的季节。但接下来还会有另一轮浪潮。”

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the August 2026 print edition with the headline “The Demons of Maryville.”
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章刊登于2026年8月印刷版，标题是《玛丽维尔的恶魔》。
